english: also = pód-pa (phod pa); jì (ji) (or frq. cì (ci)) nús-kyis (nus kyis) to one's best ability; to be able to do or to perform, dká-las (dka' las) gaṅ (gang) yaṅ (yang) mi (mi) nus (nus) he cannot perform any difficult task Thgy.; rgyál-po (rgyal po) mi (mi) nus (nus) he cannot be a king; to venture, to dare, gro (gro) nús-pa (nus pa) one that dared to go. (In W. *túb-pa* (*thub pa*) is used almost exclusively instead of it.) — 2. adj. able, nús-pa (nus pa) su (su) čé-ba (che ba) lta (lta) let us see who is more able, more efficient, who can do more, Mil.; C. also active, diligent, assiduous. — 3. sbst. power, ability, faculty, capability, c. genit.: ṅai (ngai) nús-pa-la (nus pa la) brtén-nas (brten nas) by my power, through my agency (you shall obtain it) Mil.; rtsíg-pai (rtsig pai) nús-pa (nus pa) yód-dam (yod dam) med (med) whether there will be a capability of building . . . Glr.; *dé (de) čós-la (chos la) nús-pa (nus pa) med* (med) W. this religion has no power; nús-pa (nus pa) bšíg-pa (bshig pa) ṭams-čád (thams cad) all the destructive powers; byéḍ-nus-pa (byed nus pa), stón-nus-pa (ston nus pa) the capability of doing, of showing Thgy.; rnam-smín-nus-pa (rnam smin nus pa) the power of retributive justice (Nemesis, as it were) Mil.; efficiency, efficacy, virtue (of a remedy), smán-nus (sman nus) joms ('joms) they hinder the efficacy of the medicines Med.; nús-pa (nus pa) smín (smin) the efficacy becomes complete Mil.; in a more particular sense: the effect of a medicine in the stomach (opp. to its taste etc.); there are eight different effects: lc̀i (lci), snum (snum), bsil (bsil), rtul (rtul), yaṅ (yang), rtsub (rtsub), tsa (tsha), rno (rno) S.g.; nús-pa (nus pa) gnyis (gnyis) daṅ (dang) ldan (ldan) they have both qualities S.g.; nus-stóbs (nus stobs) = nús-pa (nus pa) Sch. II. pf. of nú-ba (nu ba).
num. figure: 102.
meadow, grass-plot, green-sward, B., C., W.
aunt, the father's sister, or wife of the mother's brother.
meadow, green-sward, C., W.
parrot.
(v. ner-ba (ner ba)), W. sediment, settlings, dregs.
Sch.: 'mouse-hawk', a species of large hawk or vulture, differing from gó-bo (go bo), frequently to be met with in Kullu, but not in Ladak.
Sch. mason's trowel, ne-we (ne we) rgyag-pa (rgyag pa) to plaster, to roughcast.
= neu-(y)siṅ (ne'u (y)sing).
W. col. for lén-pa (len pa), to take, lay hold of, seize; to take out, off, away; to hold.
denotes a nodding, waving, or rocking motion, Mil.; cf. nems (nems) and snem (snem).
doubt, error Sch.

Stg. describes an elastic floor in the following manner: rkáṅ-pa (rkang pa) bżag-na (bzhag na) ni (ni) nems (nems) šes (shes) byéḍ-de (byed de), rkáṅ-pa (rkang pa) btégs-na (btegs na) ni (ni) spar (spar) żes (zhes) byed (byed): hence nems (nems), it sinks a little, gives way.
Lex. = na-mnyám (na mnyam) one of the same age, coetaneous, contemporary; Sch.: neu-ldáṅ (ne'u ldang) friend, and neu-ldáṅs (ne'u ldangs) protector, defender.
Hindi नेवला, Ssk. नकुल, ichneumon, Herpestes Pharaonis, Liš.; represented in B. as a fabulous animal, cat-like and vomiting jewels.
1. C. = ne-ṭaṅ (ne thang). — 2. grass-plots on high mountains, alpine pastures (C. spaṅ (spang)).
to sink, to fall gradually, mtíl-la (mtil la) to the bottom, = núb-pa (nub pa).
= *ne-ré* (*ne re*) W.
1. W. for nú-bo (nu bo). — 2. num. fig.: 132.

Ld. title of young noblemen, no-nó (no no) čén-mo (chen mo) the eldest of a nobleman's sons, bár-pa (bar pa) the second, čúṅ-se (chung se) the youngest; Sp. title of the highest magistrate of the country.
(Bal. ṅó-ṅo (ngo ngo)). W. for nu-mo (nu mo).
Sch.: cervical vertebra; hump of a camel.
prob. prov. for nág-po (nag po); nog-nóg (nog nog) very dark, deep-black.
